title: Document semantic memory behavior and rollout constraints
description: Update project documentation for the new shared semantic memory model, including how operator and workers share one DB, how `TAKT_CMD` and namespaces are used, and any backend-specific notes that should remain stable for future contributors.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains the shared project-level memory DB and namespace model.
Documentation explains operator versus worker access patterns and injected environment variables.
Documentation reflects the mandatory use of `takt memory` rather than append-only markdown memory.
Any touched docs stay consistent with the implemented CLI and onboarding behavior.
conflict_risks:
None — only runner.py modified; no overlap with sibling beads' expected files.
src/agent_takt/memory.py is fully implemented here. Sibling beads should import from it but not modify it. If any sibling bead lists memory.py in expected_files they should be scoped to additive imports only.
None — all changes were confined to src/agent_takt/memory.py which is not shared with other in-progress beads.
No file-scope conflicts expected. The only changed file is scaffold.py, which is not touched by any sibling bead.
None — only `src/agent_takt/memory.py` was modified; no overlapping file claims with sibling beads expected.
None — only `planner.py` and `misc.py` were changed; no overlap with other in-progress beads.
